name: Chili Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 pound dried red beans, rinsed and drained
1 green bell pepper, seeded and chopped
1 cup shredded Monterey jack cheese, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Place beans in a large bowl; add 4 cups of water and let stand overnight.
Alternatively, bring beans and 4 cups of water to a boil in a 4-quart kettle,
boil briskly for 2 minutes, then remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 1 hour.
2. In a large, deep pot or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Brown the spare ribs
well in oil on all sides. Add green pepper, onions, and garlic; brown lightly.
Add remaining 3 cups of water, bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer until meat is tender (1-1/2 to 2 hours).
3. Remove spare ribs from the pan. Slice the meat from the bones and return it to the cooking liquid in chunks; discard the bones and fat.
(You may refrigerate the stock overnight, then skim off fat and reheat.)
4. Add soaked beans and their liquid, salt, chili powder, and cumin; bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer until beans are almost tender (about 1-1/2 hours).
5. Mix in tomato sauce. Cook until beans are very tender (about 1 hour longer).
6. Serve sprinkled with jack cheese.
